key_points: Most dorms contain lead paint; only post-1978 buildings are exempt
Room assignments can change unexpectedly, leaving students without roommates
Bring a large box fan — dorms get hot and lack adequate ventilation
key_quotes: Almost all Brown dorms contain lead paint… all dorms built before 1978 contain lead paint
I was assigned a double in Metcalf with a roommate, but now apparently I don't have one anymore
Bring one of them big ol' box fans that you can put in your window. It will be hot.

key_points: Social scene described as 'what you make of it' — requires active effort
Reputation as most laid-back, fun Ivy; weed culture openly referenced
Greek life exists but not dominant; Brown leans toward clubs and independent socializing
key_quotes: I'll fit right into Brown, they smoke a ton of weed there and the grades are known to be crazy inflated
I'm currently at Brown and it's as amazing as he made it seem. I've never been stressed a day in my life
i'm triple majoring, and am pretty sure we probably have similar workloads—i have time to 🍃 at least 3 times a week and i go out every single weekend

key_points: A student-made app ('Bruno Bites') exists specifically to fix Brown's clunky dining menu system
New ramen shop on Thayer only sold 2 bowls opening day — off-campus dining competitive
No strong praise or condemnation of dining halls in student posts
key_quotes: Last semester a friend and I were lamenting the excessive number of taps and scrolls past the salad bar it takes just to see the Ratty menu
Today I only sold 2 bowls. I really want to make something Brown & RISD students actually love.
I made an app to try and fix that

key_points: December 2025 campus shooting caused severe, ongoing trauma across student body
Providence Center offered community counseling; free trauma support groups formed post-shooting
Students report anxiety and stress, but Brown's open curriculum reduces grade pressure vs. other Ivies
key_quotes: Texting all my friends to make sure they're alive is not something I have ever wanted to do
I love my school. It's my first semester here. I'm heartbroken and scared for what moving on looks like
I was a dual student at Brown/RISD… I have time to go out every single weekend. I love my schedule and feel very relaxed

key_points: Need-blind for domestic students; families under ~$65k pay nothing in tuition
International students face steep costs; one chose Brown over Oxford for need-blind aid
90k/year sticker price without aid; some students debate Brown vs. free CUNY Honors
key_quotes: I only applied to Brown for financial aid, since it was need blind
Is Brown Worth Going 90k into debt for?
if your family makes less than $65,000 a year, you pay nothing in tuition

key_points: Open Curriculum is real and genuinely distinctive — but Brown markets it relentlessly
Grade inflation is widely acknowledged; easier to get high GPA than at peer Ivies
Over 20% of undergrads registered as disabled — accommodation culture is significant
key_quotes: Brown: We have an open curriculum… Open Curriculum, and world-class research facilities… oh, we forgot to mention, Open Curriculum
The grades are known to be crazy inflated
At Brown and Harvard, more than 20 percent of undergraduates are registered as disabled

key_points: ResLife ran out of singles even for students with documented medical accommodations
Security review launched after shooting — Barus & Holley had no lobby security guards
Brown struck deal with Trump administration on DEI; drew significant student backlash
key_quotes: The res life office says it has run out of singles and cannot give me one
Barus & Holley is one of the few buildings on campus without security guards in the lobby
Do you think Brown 'sold their soul' by agreeing a deal with Trump?

key_points: Providence is underrated — walkable College Hill, close to RISD, vibrant enough
Campus is visually beautiful; students consistently praise aesthetics in tour videos
Brown IDs still use swipe cards in 2025 — no tap chips, widely criticized as outdated
key_quotes: Why are we still swiping in 2025? Tap access would save so much time and is the norm at a lot of other schools
The best Brown campus tour vlog I've seen
I really like Brown, especially the libraries

key_points: Big Tech almost absent from Brown's career fair — no Google, Meta, Apple, Microsoft in 2025
Alumni network strong for medicine and politics; one student interning at White House
CS and pre-med students should be aware of limited on-campus recruiting from top tech firms
key_quotes: Big tech? Basically just Amazon. That's it. No Google, Meta, Netflix, Apple, Microsoft
I've never been stressed a day in my life and I'll be interning at the White House this summer
Brown sends 80% of pre-med students to medical school

key_points: Full cost ~$90k/year without aid; debt load can be extreme without financial support
Grade inflation and open curriculum may reduce academic rigor relative to cost
Strong value if you qualify for need-based aid; poor value paying full price for limited tech recruiting
key_quotes: Is Brown Worth Going 90k into debt for?
Brown is still more expensive. (Brown 22k/yr vs. Penn 13k/year)
They must be really hurting for money to be promoting these cash-grab grad programs

key_points: Genuinely quirky, creative, and low-pressure compared to other Ivies
Community showed remarkable solidarity after December 2025 campus shooting
Attracts self-directed, eclectic students; poor fit for those needing structure
key_quotes: Brown feels like the only Ivy league that still feels like an Ivy league. Genuine focus on the liberal arts
I'm a Brown grad. Definitely free spirited and have random interests
Is there a type of person that WOULDN'T fit in at Brown?

key_points: **Safety**: December 2025 mass shooting killed 2, injured 8; suspect was former grad student
**Infrastructure**: Nearly all dorms contain lead paint; swipe-only ID access flagged as security gap
**Career**: Big Tech recruiting nearly nonexistent at campus career fair for CS students
key_quotes: At least 2 killed and several more hurt in shooting at Brown University in Rhode Island
Almost all Brown dorms contain lead paint
Big tech? Basically just Amazon. That's it. No Google, Meta, Netflix, Apple, Microsoft

key_points: Brown/RISD dual degree lets students triple-major across two elite schools simultaneously
Free student subscriptions include Headspace, The Atlantic, and NYT — widely unknown
Open Curriculum lets you genuinely design your education with no distribution requirements
key_quotes: I just learned students get free Headspace, The Atlantic, and NYT subscriptions
Brown University and Rhode Island School of Design have partnered… a computer science degree from Brown, and an animation degree from RISD
How cool is Brown about being gay? — one of the most liberal college campuses
